2a · Mid price & depth within ±5 bps

Simple mid = (best_bid+best_ask)/2 (left axis). Shaded areas = USD notional resting within ±5 bps of mid, bids up / asks down (right axis). 1s grid, forward-filled.

2b · Mid price & depth within ±10 bps

Simple mid = (best_bid+best_ask)/2 (left axis). Shaded areas = USD notional resting within ±10 bps of mid, bids up / asks down (right axis). 1s grid, forward-filled.

2c · Mid price & depth within ±20 bps

Simple mid = (best_bid+best_ask)/2 (left axis). Shaded areas = USD notional resting within ±20 bps of mid, bids up / asks down (right axis). 1s grid, forward-filled.

2d · Mid price & depth within ±50 bps

Simple mid = (best_bid+best_ask)/2 (left axis). Shaded areas = USD notional resting within ±50 bps of mid, bids up / asks down (right axis). 1s grid, forward-filled. Note: the ±50bps band reaches past the captured 20 levels 3% of the time, so depth here is a lower bound in those moments.

2e · Mid price & depth within ±100 bps

Simple mid = (best_bid+best_ask)/2 (left axis). Shaded areas = USD notional resting within ±100 bps of mid, bids up / asks down (right axis). 1s grid, forward-filled. Note: the ±100bps band reaches past the captured 20 levels 33% of the time, so depth here is a lower bound in those moments.

3a · Bid side — cumulative depth by level

Each row is a level (0 = best bid). Color = cumulative USD notional summed from the best bid down to that level. Log color scale.

3b · Ask side — cumulative depth by level

Each row is a level (0 = best ask). Color = cumulative USD notional summed from the best ask up to that level. Log color scale.

4 · Resting depth by price (combined map)

Classic depth map: y = price, color = USD notional resting in each price bin, mid price drawn in white. Log color scale.
